name: Firmware Root of Trust
description: A firmware root of trust verifies early firmware before the device starts.
This can be provided by processor and platform features such as Intel BootGuard, AMD Platform Secure Boot, HP SureStart, or by firmware implementations such as coreboot verified boot.
failure-impact: If fwupd cannot detect a firmware root of trust then it cannot verify that early firmware is protected from tampering before the operating system starts.

failure-results.unknown: firmware root of trust was not detected

resolution: Contact your OEM, who may be able to issue a firmware update.
